commentary_text: The model backs a Switzerland win at 77% while the sportsbook consensus sits at 60%—a 16-percentage-point gap favoring the model's conviction. The market also assigns Bosnia and Herzegovina a 16-percentage-point higher win probability than the model does. This hedging pattern suggests the market may be pricing in information the model cannot see: recent lineup changes, injury absences among key players, or form shifts since the model's last update could all be tempering confidence in Switzerland despite their structural rating advantage. The dashboard surfaces this divergence as a signal worth monitoring, not as evidence that either source has misread the matchup.
